_doc: Single source of truth for NYT subject-tag merging, filtering, and headline-event classification. Used by build_data.py, patch_beats.py, build_state_keywords.py, and index.html. When you change merges/filters here, run scripts/patch_beats.py to propagate them into already-built data files. Keys beginning with '_doc' are ignored by all loaders.
_doc_subject_merges: Map of stale/variant subject tags to their canonical form. Single-word ALL-CAPS variants need explicit entries (auto-titlecase rule only fires for multi-word tags). Old NYT terminology gets remapped to current preferred form. Run patch_beats.py after editing.
_doc_abbrev_fixes: Restore state/country abbreviations that get mangled by Python's str.title() when applied to multi-word ALL-CAPS tags (e.g. '(Washington, DC)' → '(Washington, Dc)'). Applied as a post-titlecase pass and also as one-time merges for already-corrupted on-disk forms.
_doc_filters: generic_subjects_always_filter and generic_prefixes_always_filter apply EVERYWHERE — beats tab, reporter popups, state popups, Excel. state_coverage_generic_subjects is an ADDITIONAL filter applied only in the state coverage view (generic election/government tags every state has).
_doc_headline_events: A tag is a 'headline event' (vs. recurring theme) when its NAME structurally indicates a specific dated incident — NOT when its coverage merely clusters in time. We do NOT use statistical year-spike detection because it conflates 'topic that became prominent' (e.g. In Vitro Fertilization in 2024) with 'specific dated event' (e.g. Hurricane Ian 2022). Detection rules: (1) tag contains a single 4-digit year not part of a range, (2) tag matches a substring pattern (named storm, etc.), (3) tag is in the curated list. 'Primaries and Caucuses' is intentionally NOT in headline events — it's a recurring theme for early-primary states.

subject_merges.Freedom and Human Rights: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

_doc_year_detection: A tag is also classified as a headline event if its name contains a 4-digit year (1900–2099) NOT part of a range. Range markers are '-' or '–' immediately after the year. So 'Hurricane Sandy (2012)' → headline (single year), but 'World War II (1939-45)' → recurring (year range = era), 'Iraq War (2003- )' → recurring (open-ended era), 'Coronavirus (2019-nCoV)' → recurring (year is part of identifier, not a single event marker).
